question: What will be the voter turnout percentage in the July 11, 2026 Johor state election?

factors.description: Analyst consensus for Johor 2026 clusters 60-70%, mode ~64%: Tawfik & ST 'below 65%', Mazlan Ali 65-70%, Md Akbal 'at least 60%'. Directly Johor-specific.
Recent non-Covid Malaysian standalone state elections: Melaka 2021 65.9%, 2023 six-state 65-73%. Suggests upside toward 70.
2022 Johor was 54.9% but Covid-depressed; no Covid now, Saturday poll, PH arranging transport for Singapore-based voters. Upward pressure vs 2022.
Structural Johor drag: ~300k voters in Singapore, Undi18 roll expansion (47.6% under 40 vote less), 'ghost of 2022' apathy. Downward pressure.

summary: Standalone Johor state election (PRN Johor), July 11 2026, with no Covid restrictions. Johor-specific analyst consensus clusters 60-70% (Tawfik & Straits Times "below 65%", Mazlan Ali 65-70%, Md Akbal "at least 60%"), centered ~64%. Recent non-Covid standalone Malaysian state elections (Melaka 2021 65.9%, 2023 six-state polls 65-73%) suggest upside potential, but Johor has unique structural drag: ~300k voters residing in Singapore, Undi18 roll expansion (47.6% under 40, who vote less), and lingering "ghost of 2022" apathy. The 2022 low of 54.9% was Covid-depressed and used only as a downside tail. Modeled as a scenario mixture: 50% base N(64,3.2), 27% apathy downside N(58.5,2.5), 23% youth-mobilization upside N(70,3). Median 63.7%, P10 57.4%, P90 70.8%.
reasoning:
My forecast for the July 11, 2026 Johor state election turnout:

**Median: 63.7%** (P10: 57.4% — P90: 70.8%)

**Key reasoning:**
- This is a **standalone** state election (not concurrent with a federal GE), which structurally runs lower than the 82-85% concurrent Johor elections of 2013/2018.
- **Johor-specific analyst consensus** clusters 60-70% (mode ~64%): several say "below 65%", one projects 65-70%, one "at least 60%."
- The 2022 record-low of **54.9% was Covid-depressed** and used only as a downside tail; no restrictions exist now, PH is subsidizing transport for Singapore-based voters, and it's a Saturday poll.
- **Recent non-Covid standalone Malaysian state elections** (Melaka 2021 65.9%, 2023 six-state 65-73%) provide upside pressure toward 70%.
- Counterbalanced by structural drag: ~300k voters in Singapore, Undi18 roll expansion (47.6% under 40, who vote at lower rates), and residual apathy.

Modeled as a three-scenario mixture (50% base ~64%, 27% apathy downside ~58.5%, 23% mobilization upside ~70%). The distribution sits comfortably within the question's 48-82 bounds, with tails reaching a 2022-style low (~54%) and a strong-youth-turnout high (~75%).
